backlog.title: List every match on the first Tab (§14.7)
backlog.description: Implement PRD §14.7 (new) + Decision 22 + the §14.1 rule-2 cross-reference: when the token being completed matches two or more candidates (skill tags OR long-form flags), the completion must surface the full filtered candidate set as visible hints on the FIRST Tab — never silently halt at the longest common prefix. The change is confined to the completion EMIT layer: bash and zsh emitted scripts set a session-global list-ambiguous-matches option (disclosed + opt-out-able); fish already lists by default. No flags are added/removed/renamed (§14.4 lockstep + usageText NOT triggered). Build on session 004's Complete+green completion machinery.

milestones.title: Emitted scripts set the list-ambiguous-matches option (disclosed + opt-out)
milestones.description: Single cohesive milestone. The §14.7 fix has two halves: (1) offer every match — already true, lock it, do not change it; (2) make the shell SHOW them on the first tab — the fix this milestone adds, via a session-global option set inside the emitted scripts. Three surfaces change (bash on-disk==emitted file, zsh derived eval registration const, README disclosure) plus byte-level test locks. T1 is the code; T2 is the test locks; T3 is the changeset-level README doc (Mode B) that runs last.

tasks.title: bash + zsh emitted scripts set the list-ambiguous-matches option
tasks.description: Add the active option-setting line + a disclosure comment + a commented opt-out to the two shells that need it. bash: edit the on-disk file completions/skilldozer.bash (which is //go:embed-ded AND emitted verbatim by runCompletion, so one edit covers both the §14.5 source path and the §14.6 eval path). zsh: edit the zshEvalRegistration const in main.go (the eval-time append produced by zshEvalScript), leaving the on-disk autoload file and the completionScript byte-identity lock untouched. fish needs NO change (lists by default).

subtasks.title: bash: add show-all-if-ambiguous to the on-disk (== emitted) script
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_d9b30e368811/architecture/code_change_map.md Touch point 1. The file completions/skilldozer.bash is currently 69 lines; its LAST line (verbatim) is `complete -F _skilldozer_completion skilldozer`. This file is //go:embed-ded (var bashCompletion, main.go:55) and runCompletion emits bash VERBATIM (main.go:1520), so a single edit covers BOTH the §14.5 manual source path and the §14.6 eval path (the bytes are identical). No Go code change is needed for bash — the embed picks up the file change on rebuild.
2. INPUT: The current completions/skilldozer.bash (skills-first completion function _skilldozer_completion + the trailing `complete -F` registration line). Build is green and all tests pass as the baseline.
3. LOGIC: After the final `complete -F _skilldozer_completion skilldozer` line, append:
   (a) a DISCLOSURE COMMENT BLOCK stating: §14.7 lists ambiguous matches on the FIRST tab; this is a readline SESSION-GLOBAL option (changes listing for every command in this shell, not just skilldozer); the default is OFF (first tab completes the common prefix and beeps, list appears only on the second tab); to restore stock behavior run `bind 'set show-all-if-ambiguous off'`.
   (b) an ACTIVE guarded line: `[[ $- == *i* ]] && bind 'set show-all-if-ambiguous on'`. The `[[ $- == *i* ]] &&` guard keeps it quiet if the file is ever sourced non-interactively (e.g. an eval test harness); `bind` in a non-interactive bash prints a warning which the guard silences. Completions only matter in interactive shells so the option still applies where it matters.
4. OUTPUT: completions/skilldozer.bash ends with the disclosure block + guarded active bind line + commented opt-out. `go build ./...` succeeds. TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk (main_test.go:3139) stays GREEN (the embed var and the on-disk file move together — same file). TestRunCompletionBashScript (main_test.go:3163) stays green (the new content is appended, `_skilldozer_completion` still present, stderr empty). After rebuild, `./skilldozer --completions --shell bash` output contains `show-all-if-ambiguous on`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The disclosure comment block appended to the script IS the §14.7 'disclose in the emitted script's comments' requirement for bash. No separate per-feature doc step — it rides with this work.

subtasks.title: zsh: add NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS to the derived eval registration
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_d9b30e368811/architecture/code_change_map.md Touch point 2. The zshEvalRegistration raw-string const is at main.go:1260. It is appended by zshEvalScript (main.go:1244) to form the DERIVED eval output; runCompletion (main.go:1499) emits this derived wrapper for zsh only (bash/fish stay verbatim). completionScript (main.go:1215) still returns the on-disk autoload file completions/_skilldozer VERBATIM — do NOT touch completionScript or completions/_skilldozer for the required fix. The const currently contains a disclosure comment + `autoload -Uz compinit` / guarded `compinit` / `compdef _skilldozer skilldozer`.
2. INPUT: main.go with the working zshEvalRegistration const + zshEvalScript + runCompletion (session 004 green baseline).
3. LOGIC: Inside the zshEvalRegistration const (before or after the compdef block), add:
   (a) a DISCLOSURE COMMENT stating: §14.7 lists ambiguous matches on the FIRST tab; this is a SESSION-GLOBAL zsh option (default LIST_AMBIGUOUS is ON, which completes the common prefix and lists only at the exact ambiguous point); NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS + the default AUTO_LIST lists all prefix matches at once; it changes listing for every command in this shell, not just skilldozer; to restore stock behavior run `setopt LIST_AMBIGUOUS`.
   (b) an ACTIVE line: `setopt NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS`.
   Keep the const a valid Go raw string literal — NO BACKTICKS inside (the existing const already observes this; use single-line or line-continued shell syntax, never a backtick).
   Broaden the zshEvalRegistration doc comment (main.go:1257) to mention it now also sets the §14.7 listing option (not just compdef). Broaden zshEvalScript / runCompletion doc comments ONLY if they claim the registration is solely about compdef — do not over-edit.
   OPTIONAL (may skip): zsh manual (fpath) path parity — add `setopt NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS` as the first line of the _skilldozer() function body in completions/_skilldozer. If pursued, completionScript('zsh') MUST remain byte-identical to that file (the edit must not depend on the derivation). SIMPLEST: leave completions/_skilldozer alone.
4. OUTPUT: `eval "$(skilldozer --completions)"` (zsh) now sets NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS at eval time. `go build ./...` succeeds. completionScript('zsh') is still byte-identical to completions/_skilldozer (the const is appended AFTER completionScript returns; the byte-identity lock is untouched). After rebuild, `./skilldozer --completions --shell zsh` output contains `setopt NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS` and the opt-out token `setopt LIST_AMBIGUOUS`.
5. DOCS: [Mode A] The disclosure comment inside the const IS the §14.7 'disclose in the emitted script's comments' requirement for zsh. The broadened doc comment on zshEvalRegistration is the developer-facing reference. No separate per-feature doc step.

tasks.title: Tests locking the emitted-byte contract
tasks.description: There is no in-process way to drive a live shell's first-Tab behavior from the skilldozer binary, so §13 acceptance gains no new in-process assertions. Instead, lock the §14.7 contract with BYTE-LEVEL assertions on the emitted scripts: does the bash/zsh output CONTAIN the active option line AND the disclosed opt-out token? Mirror the existing TestZshEvalScript* / TestRunCompletion*Script pattern. TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk must remain green (do not weaken it).

subtasks.title: Assert bash + zsh emitted output carry the option + opt-out
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_d9b30e368811/architecture/system_context.md 'Existing test patterns' and code_change_map.md Touch point 5. Existing patterns to mirror: TestRunCompletionBashScript (main_test.go:3163) runs `run([]string{"--completions","--shell","bash"}, &out, &errOut)`, asserts exit 0 + stdout contains marker + stderr empty. TestZshEvalScriptRegistersCompdef (main_test.go:3288) calls zshEvalScript(completionScript("zsh")) and asserts substrings present. TestRunCompletionZshIsEvalSafe (main_test.go:3316) runs `run([]string{"--completions"}, …)` under t.Setenv("SKILLDOZER_SHELL","zsh") and asserts substrings in the end-to-end output.
2. INPUT: main.go and completions/skilldozer.bash from P1.M1.T1.S1 + P1.M1.T1.S2 (the option lines now present in the emitted scripts).
3. LOGIC (add new tests or extend existing — exact names are the implementer's; follow the existing helpers run() / zshEvalScript() / completionScript()):
   (a) BASH: add TestRunCompletionBashListsAmbiguous (or extend TestRunCompletionBashScript at main_test.go:3163). run([]string{"--completions","--shell","bash"}, &out, &errOut) → assert code 0, errOut empty, then assert out.String() CONTAINS `show-all-if-ambiguous on` (the active line) AND contains the opt-out token `show-all-if-ambiguous off` (disclosed in the comment). Optionally assert the interactivity guard token `*i*` is present.
   (b) ZSH (unit): extend TestZshEvalScriptRegistersCompdef (main_test.go:3288) — assert zshEvalScript(completionScript("zsh")) CONTAINS `setopt NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS` (active) AND the opt-out token `setopt LIST_AMBIGUOUS` (disclosed). Add the two want-substrings to its existing loop/list.
   (c) ZSH (end-to-end): extend TestRunCompletionZshIsEvalSafe (main_test.go:3316) — under t.Setenv("SKILLDOZER_SHELL","zsh"), run([]string{"--completions"}, &out, &errOut), then assert the emitted script out.String() CONTAINS `NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS`.
   (d) BYTE-IDENTITY HOLDS: TestEmbeddedCompletionsMatchOnDisk (main_test.go:3139) must remain GREEN. For zsh it is untouched (the const lives in zshEvalRegistration, appended after completionScript returns). For bash the embed var and the on-disk file are the same file, so they move together. Do NOT weaken this test.
4. OUTPUT: `go test ./...` is 100% green. No existing green test regresses. The §14.7 emitted-byte contract is locked for bash + zsh (active option + disclosed opt-out both asserted).
5. DOCS: none — test-only change, no user-facing/config/API surface change.

tasks.title: Sync changeset-level documentation (README §14.7 disclosure)
tasks.description: Mode B changeset-level documentation: the README disclosure only makes sense once BOTH emitted scripts (bash + zsh) carry the option, so it runs last and depends on the implementing subtasks. It discloses (per §14.7 / §15) that the emitted script sets a session-global list-ambiguous-matches option, names the option per shell, notes it affects every command in that shell, and gives the opt-out one-liners.

subtasks.title: README: disclose the option set + opt-out one-liners
subtasks.context_scope:
CONTRACT DEFINITION:
1. RESEARCH NOTE: See plan/005_d9b30e368811/architecture/code_change_map.md Touch point 4. README.md's Shell completions section spans lines 290-366. The insertion point is AFTER the existing skills-first/long-form-only bullet list (which ends around line 334, right before 'Prefer to copy the file instead?' at ~line 336). The bullet list already documents `skilldozer <tab>` lists skills and `--c<tab>` lists --check/--completions.
2. INPUT: The emitted scripts from P1.M1.T1.S1 (bash show-all-if-ambiguous) and P1.M1.T1.S2 (zsh NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS) are in place; the test locks from P1.M1.T2.S1 are green.
3. LOGIC: After the existing bullet list (and before the 'Prefer to copy the file instead?' paragraph), add a short DISCLOSURE PARAGRAPH + OPT-OUT block:
   (a) State that the emitted completion script sets a SESSION-GLOBAL option so AMBIGUOUS MATCHES LIST ON THE FIRST TAB instead of halting at the common prefix.
   (b) NAME the option per shell: bash `show-all-if-ambiguous`, zsh `NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS`. Note fish already lists by default — no option is set for fish.
   (c) Note this affects listing for EVERY command in that shell, not just skilldozer, and that it is set only when you load skilldozer completions (via eval/source).
   (d) Give the OPT-OUT one-liners in a fenced code block:
       bash — restore the default (list on second tab): `bind 'set show-all-if-ambiguous off'`
       zsh — restore the default (list at the exact ambiguous point): `setopt LIST_AMBIGUOUS`
   Preserve all existing README content (eval one-liners, flag list, manual copy paths, fish source line). Use the README's existing tone (mirror mcpeepants style, §15).
4. OUTPUT: README Shell completions section discloses the option + opt-out. Verify: `grep -q 'show-all-if-ambiguous' README.md && grep -q 'NO_LIST_AMBIGUOUS' README.md && grep -q 'LIST_AMBIGUOUS' README.md`. The existing README structure is intact.
5. DOCS: This IS the Mode B changeset-level documentation task for this delta. It depends on the implementing subtasks and runs last. It fulfills the §14.7 / §15 'disclose in the README' half of the requirement (the other half — disclosure in the emitted script comments — rode with T1 as Mode A).
